summary: Partial decompilation of the ftCo_ItemGet action cluster. The PR added a new C translation unit and header for src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ItemGet, moved most of the corresponding code out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, wired the new object into obj_files.mk, and updated fighter/item headers and include sites needed by item pickup and heavy-item interactions. The reported file match was 99.25%, with many functions at 100% and several remaining partials or inline-assembly fallbacks.

key_files.role: New C implementation for the ftCo_ItemGet action/item pickup functions, including C bodies and MUST_MATCH inline-assembly fallbacks.
New public header declaring ftCo_80094150, ftCo_800942A0, ftCo_8009447C, ftCo_80094694, ftCo_800948A8, ftCo_ItemGet_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll, and related helpers.
Large removal of existing assembly for the item-get range, leaving only a small remainder.
Added ftCo_ItemGet.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES so the new C unit replaces the removed assembly coverage.
Refined fighter/item pickup related types, including itPickup offsets and Item_GObj pointer typing.
Received remaining inline asm functions ft_80094098 and ft_80094138 after they were removed from ft_08A4.s.
Renamed vector squared-magnitude macros to DOT-style names and changed PSVEC prototypes from f32 to float.
Changed it_8026B2B4 return type from bool to enum_t to support item-get light/heavy branching.
Renamed Item_8026AB54 parameters to owner_gobj and part.
Removed pickup assert strings that were moved/duplicated into the new ftCo_ItemGet.c context.

what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ItemGet.c with 1245 new lines covering item-get detection, pickup execution, animation/physics/collision callbacks, and state-transition helpers.
Removed roughly 1040 lines of assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, converting most of that range to C or local inline asm guarded by matching macros.

PR report shows src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ItemGet.c at 99.25% file match: ftCo_80094150, ftCo_800948A8, ftCo_80094694, ft_80094818, ft_80094790, ftCo_80094D90, ftCo_80094DF8, ftCo_ItemGet_Coll, ftCo_ItemGet_Phys, and ftCo_ItemGet_IASA were reported as 100%.
Left some functions partially matched according to the report: ftCo_ItemGet_Anim at 94.48%, ftCo_800942A0 at 98.87%, ftCo_8009447C at 99.22%, and ftCo_80094B6C at 99.89%.
Created ftCo_ItemGet.h and added includes for it across common attacks, Donkey heavy item states, Fox, Game & Watch, Link, Peach, fighter.c, and related fighter files.
Added mv.co.itemget.x0 to ftCommon motion vars for item-get state-specific data.
Refined itPickup from anonymous offset fields into named Vec2 arrays: gr_light_offset[2], gr_heavy_offset[2], and air_light_offset[2].
Changed it_8026B2B4 from bool to enum_t, matching its use as an item category/light-heavy selector rather than a pure boolean.
Moved ftItemPickup_803C5580 and assert strings out of ftitempickup.c into ftCo_ItemGet.c under MWERKS_GEKKO/MUST_MATCH handling.
Added the new object file to obj_files.mk.
smart_moves: Used a mixed strategy: functions that matched were implemented in C, while hard cases retained inline assembly under `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` with C alternatives under `#else` for readability and future cleanup.
Separated the new item-get action into its own ftCommon source/header instead of leaving the whole range in generic ft_08A4.s, improving searchability and future decomp locality.
Named the itPickup layout around behaviorally meaningful pickup regions, making item pickup range checks much easier to reason about than raw x0/x8/x10 offsets.
Recognized that it_8026B2B4 was used as more than a boolean and retconned its prototype to enum_t, which better matches branches comparing values 0 and 1.
Kept exact match-only data/strings under MWERKS_GEKKO and force_active pragmas where needed, reducing risk of data ordering or string emission regressions.
Added many precise include dependencies rather than relying on broad or stale forward declarations.
decomp_lessons: When an action file is only partially matchable, it is still useful to land the file with inline-asm fallbacks and C alternatives for the remaining difficult functions.
Large asm-to-C conversions often expose real struct layout: here the pickup offsets became a named itPickup structure and motion vars gained an itemget substruct.
A helper named like a predicate may still need enum_t if assembly compares its return against multiple values; verify call-site comparisons before committing to bool.

Item pickup logic depends on fighter facing direction, ground/air state, light/heavy pickup offsets, item position, and item x/y ranges; preserving local variable ordering and temporary scopes can matter for matching.
Keep exact assert strings and file-name strings close to the decompiled code that emits them, guarded with MWERKS/MUST_MATCH pragmas if data placement matters.
Build-system wiring is part of the match: adding the C object to obj_files.mk must accompany removal of assembly coverage.
naming_conventions: New common action files follow `ftCo_<Action>.c` and `ftCo_<Action>.h`, as in `ftCo_ItemGet.c` and `ftCo_ItemGet.h`.
Motion variable naming used a nested union member `mv.co.itemget.x0` with an explicitly named `struct ftCo_ItemGetVars`.
Pickup offsets were renamed to directional/weight names: `gr_light_offset`, `gr_heavy_offset`, and `air_light_offset`.
The PR preserved unknown address-based symbols such as `ftCo_800942A0`, `ftCo_8009447C`, `ftCo_80094694`, and `ftCo_80094B6C` where semantic names were not established.
Header prototypes include address comments such as `/* 0945B8 */ void ftCo_ItemGet_Anim(...)`, maintaining the project’s address-indexed documentation style.
Parameter names were clarified where evidence allowed, e.g. `Item_8026AB54(HSD_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* owner_gobj, u8 part)`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s in bulk and replaced it with C in a new translation unit.
For several unresolved functions, used inline `asm` bodies gated by `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` and provided normal C under `#else`.
Used dummy unused stack declarations under MUST_MATCH, e.g. `u8 _[8] = { 0 };`, to influence stack layout.
Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma pop`, and `#pragma force_active on` around match-sensitive inline asm and static string data.
Left exact assembly labels in inline asm blocks while using C signatures for the surrounding functions.
Used small static inline helpers like `inlineA0` and `inlineB0` in C alternatives to shape codegen and isolate repeated behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice, so there is no evidence of reviewer-requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched ftCo_ItemGet_Anim, ftCo_800942A0, ftCo_8009447C, or ftCo_80094B6C.
Search for later renames of ftCo_80094150 and ftCo_800942A0 to semantic item pickup names.
Check whether itPickup offset names were reused or refined in later fighter/item decomp work.
Look for follow-up PRs removing MUST_MATCH inline asm from ftCo_ItemGet.c.
Search for later changes to it_8026B2B4 return type or naming.
